prerequisites: A Bunny account that is reachable at your account subdomain, in the form https://<subdomain>.bunny.com.
Your account subdomain value, used to build the API base URL https://<subdomain>.bunny.com.
An API Key for your Bunny account, used as the bearer token for authentication.
Optionally, a start date in ISO-8601 UTC format (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ) to bound how far back data is synced.

authMethods.name: API Key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates every request with a single API Key, supplied via the required secret config field 'apikey'. It is sent as an HTTP Bearer token in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <apikey>). No OAuth or other credentials are used.

setupSteps.title: Identify your Bunny subdomain
setupSteps.body: Determine the subdomain specific to your Bunny account or service. The connector builds its API base URL as https://<subdomain>.bunny.com, so the subdomain must match the host you use to reach your Bunny account. Enter this value in the required 'subdomain' config field.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a Bunny API Key
setupSteps.body: Generate an API Key for your Bunny account from the Bunny dashboard/developer settings. This key is the credential the connector uses to authenticate against the Bunny developer API at your subdomain. Treat it as a secret.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Provide the API Key in the required secret field 'apikey' and the account subdomain in the required field 'subdomain'. The connector sends the key as a Bearer token in the Authorization header on each request.

setupSteps.title: Set an optional start date
setupSteps.body: Optionally set 'start_date' to limit how far back records are synced. It must be a UTC datetime in the format YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (for example 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Leave it empty to use the connector default.
